mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trajectory in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of co-occurring positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improved supply chain normalization and capacity utilization, reducing input cost pressures and supporting corporate margins, thereby boosting equity prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO readings consistently correlate with negative or flat 20-day SPX returns over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains above 4.0%, real interest rates turn deeply positive, compressing equity valuations and triggering negative forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where both inflation and policy rates exceed the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized rate demonstrates robust aggregate demand, which historically supports corporate profit growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth versus 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment stability indicate a resilient labor market that sustains wage-driven consumption, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns in more than 60% of months where both series show positive momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and future economic slowdown, leading to negative equity returns over a three-month horizon as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances following a 3-month HOUST decline exceeding 5%.
